observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: 1. Direct the City General Services Officer (CGSO) to maintain the Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and
2. Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; and
3. Prepare a lapsing schedule records for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive (LCE):
a. Create an Inventory Committee and conduct the physical count of all
PPEs owned by the city following COA Circular No. 2020-006;
b. Require the committee to submit the RPCPPE on or before January 31
of each year to the COA Auditor; and
c. Require the GSO and Accounting Office to undertake collaborative
procedures to ensure that all PPEs included in the RPCPPE are duly
recorded in their respective records and that the property Cards (PCs)
maintained by the GSO and the PPELCs maintained by the Accounting
Office are reconciled.

We recommended that LCE instruct the General Service Officer to update
the ARE/PAR of all the properties and equipment issued by the LGU.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that Local Chief
Executive:
a. Require the Inventory Committee to conduct an inventory of all the road
networks owned by the City and prepare the Report on the Physical
Count of the Local Road Network;
b. Direct the GSO to maintain Local Road Network Property Card for all
roads and its components; and
c. Require the City Accountant the following reporting procedures:
i. Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road
components for every road network; and
ii. Prepare a lapsing schedule records for the computation of the
depreciation for each component at the end of the year.

We recommended that LCE:
a. Instruct the Treasurer’s Office to prepare and maintain Real Property
Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card; and
b. Instruct the Accountant to establish the Receivables based on the Real
Property Tax Account Register.

We recommended that LCE require the BAC Chairperson to faithfully
adhere to the prescribed days of sending invitations to the Auditor’s Office to
avoid conflict of schedules and ensure their attendance for every phase/stage
of procurement activity.

We recommended that Management faithfully monitor the implementation of
all planned LDRRM PPAs to ensure the achievements of the City’s goals and
objectives in mitigating the impact of the disaster.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the DF Focal Person to conduct
regular coordination with the implementing offices to ensure that PPAs are
undertaken within the period.

We recommended that the City Accountant trace the period or date of acquisition of the assets and compute the depreciation based on the estimated useful life of assets as provided under COA Circular 2003-007.
5. Direct the Accounting Office to coordinate with the concerned Implementing Office, trace the source agencies and provide for the utilization report(s) pertaining thereto;

6. Instruct the City Accountant to return the excess funds to its Source Agency. Henceforth, ensure that all excess funds from fund transfers are returned to the SA upon completion of the intended projects as required in COA Circular 1994-103.
We recommended that Management submit to the Auditor a Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and complete documentary requirements related thereto.

We recommended that Management instruct the City General Services Officer to completely fill-up all the POs with the required essential information before acceptance and payment thereof and henceforth furnish the Office of the Auditor copies of the said POs within five (5) working days upon its perfection.

9. Conduct in-depth analysis and verification of the receivables and determine those deemed uncollectible after all the efforts have been exerted, for possible write off in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005; and

10. Instruct the Accounting Department to prepare quarterly a statement scheduling overdue accounts receivable for the agency head or its authorized representative to review and sign the statement to ensure that he has examined report and has instituted, or will institute, action for their collection.

We recommended that Management require the concerned accountable officers to submit immediately the paid DVs and their supporting documents to the Auditor and henceforth accomplish all the necessary or required items therein in conformity with the provisions set in Volume II of GAM for LGUs.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with the proper charging and the required reports and documentations for the different transactions be paid through the petty cash fund in accordance and compliance with COA Circulars No. 2012-001 and 2013-001.

We recommended that City Accountant submit the lacking required documents corresponding to each transaction above and henceforth, adhere faithfully to Section 4(6) of PD 1445 to avoid Notice of Suspensions.

We recommended that management should furnish COA copy of any contracts or purchase orders including all pertinent supporting documents as enumerated under COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 within 5 days after its perfection or approval.

1.6 We recommended that Management:
a. Direct the City General Services Officer to maintain the Local Road
Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and
b. Require the City Accountant the following reporting procedures:
i. Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road
components for every road network; and
ii. Prepare a lapsing schedule records for the computation of the
depreciation for each component at the end of the year.

2.6 We recommended that the City Accountant trace the period or date of
acquisition of the assets and compute the depreciation based on the
estimated useful life of assets as provided under COA Circular 2003-007.

3.7 We recommended that Management:
a. Direct the Accounting Office to coordinate with the concerned
Implementing Office, trace the source agencies and provide for the
utilization reports pertaining thereto; and
b. Instruct the City Accountant to return the excess funds to its Source
Agency. Henceforth, ensure that all excess funds from fund transfers
are returned to the SA upon completion of the intended projects as
required in COA Circular 1994-103.

4.7 We recommended that Management submit to the Auditor the Monthly
Report of Fuel Consumption and complete documentary requirements
related thereto.

5.12 We recommended that Management instruct the City General Services
Officer to completely filled-up all the POs with the required essential
information before acceptance and payment thereof and henceforth
furnish the Auditor copies of the said POs within five (5) working days
upon perfection.

6.5 We recommended that Management:
a. Conduct in-depth analysis and verification of the receivables and
determine those deemed uncollectible after all the efforts have been
exerted, for possible write off in accordance with COA Circular No.
2016-005; and
b. Instruct the Accounting Department to prepare quarterly a
statement scheduling overdue accounts receivable for the agency
head or its authorized representative to review and sign the statement to ensure that he has examined report and has instituted, or will institute, action for their collection.

7.10 We recommended that Management require the concerned accountable
officers to submit immediately the paid DVs and their supporting
documents to the Auditor and henceforth accomplish all the necessary or
required items therein in conformity with the provisions set forth in
Volume II of GAM for LGUs.

8.7 The Management commented that some offices were granted to establish PCF
fund to cater their minimal expenses on some petty items needed or required
by the respective Offices. And henceforth will strictly comply with COA
Circulars No. 2012-001 and 2013-001 for future transactions.

9.7 We recommended that the City Accountant submit the lacking required
documents corresponding to each transaction above and henceforth,
adhere faithfully to Section 4(6) of PD 1445 to avoid receipt of Notice of
Suspensions.

We recommended that the Honorable City Mayor::
1. Direct the City General Service Officer to submit to the City Accountant the related AIR, duly accomplished and signed by the Property and Inspecting Officers, as basis in the recognizing the receipt of the transferred 200 e-trike units.
2. Direct the City Accountant to record the receipt of the e-trike units by debiting the Motor Vehicles account and crediting Government Equity account. Prepare the related Journal Entry Voucher, copy of which to be submitted to DOE.

We recommended that the Honorable City Mayor:
3. Direct the City Engineer to facilitate the immediate setting up of charging stations to the designated areas mentioned in the deployment plan.
4. Require the City Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer, who is assigned to provide overall management of the deployment project, to ensure that the deployment stage is regularly monitored and supervised for the immediate operation of the e-trike units.

We recommended that the Honorable City Mayor:
5. Direct the City Treasurer to open a Current Account solely for Special Trust Fund-QRF.
6. Facilitate the actual transfer of the unspent/unexpended balances of the QRF and DRRM from the General Fund to the new STF-DRRM QRF account.

We recommended that the Honorable City Mayor:
7. Require the City Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office to prepare and submit to the Office of the Auditor an annual LDRRMFIP for CY 2019 in adherence to the provisions set-forth in COA Circular No. 2012-002 for auditorial review and evaluation.

We recommended that management should furnish COA copy of any contract/ purchase order including all pertinent supporting documents as enumerated under COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 within five (5) days after its perfection / approval.

We recommended that the Honorable City Mayor:
9. Instruct and supervise the City Treasurer and all local accountable officer to faithfully adhere to the prescribed regulations in preparing and submission of monthly reports of accountability for accountable forms to the Office of the Auditor.

1.6 We recommended that the Honorable Mayor:
a. Direct the City General Service Officer to submit to the City
Accountant the related AIR, duly accomplished and signed by the
Property and Inspecting Officers, as basis in the recognizing the
receipt of the transferred 200 e-trike units; and
b. Direct the City Accountant to record the receipt of the e-trike units
by debiting the Motor Vehicles account and crediting Government
Equity account. Prepare the related Journal Entry Voucher, copy of
which to be submitted to DOE.

2.7 We recommended that Honorable City Mayor:
a. Direct the City Engineer to facilitate the immediate setting up of
charging stations to the designated areas mentioned in the
deployment plan; and
b. Require the City Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer, who
is assigned to provide overall management of the deployment
project, to ensure that the deployment stage is regularly monitored
and supervised for the immediate operation of the e-trike units.

3.7 We recommended that Management:
a) Direct the City Treasurer to open a Current Account solely for
Special Trust Fund-QRF; and
b) Facilitate the actual transfer of the unspent/unexpended balances
of the QRF and DRRM from the General Fund to the new STF-DRRM
QRF account.

4.6 We recommended that the Honorable City Mayor require the City Disaster
Risk Reduction Management Office to prepare and submit to the Auditor
an annual LDRRMFIP for CY 2019 in adherence to the provisions set-forth
in COA Circular No. 2012-002 for auditorial review and evaluation.

5.5 We reiterated our previous recommendation that Management furnish
the Auditor a copy of any contract/purchase order including all pertinent
supporting documents thereto as mandated in COA Circular 2009-001
dated February 12, 2009 within five (5) days after its perfection and/or
approval.

6.5 We recommended that Management instruct and supervise the City
Accountant, the City Treasurer and all other concerned officers to
faithfully comply and observe timely submission of reports to the Office
of the Auditor in order not to delay the auditorial review of transactions.

7.6 We recommended that the Honorable City Mayor instruct and supervise
the City Treasurer and all local accountable officer to faithfully adhere to
the prescribed regulations in preparing and submission of monthly
reports of accountability for accountable forms to the Auditor.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct:
1. the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of PPE; prepare the inventory report using the Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE): and submit a copy to the Audit Team.
2. the City Accountant to update all property records; maintain PPE Ledger Card for each category of PPE. Such ledger cards shall contain the details of the PPE account in the inventory control account in the general ledger to ensure accurate presentation of PPE in the financial statements.
3. the CGSO to maintain Property Cards (PC) and Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) for properties and equipment to account for the receipt and disposition of the same to establish custodianship and to determine accountability.
4. the City Accountant and the CGSO to reconcile their records with the physical count report.

5. We recommended that the City Accountant to make a detailed review of the unsupported dormant account balance reflected in the books of accounts and effect the necessary adjustments thereof.
6. We further recommended to make a request for write-off for the account balance with a valid justification on the reason that the said account balances should be written off, such as (a) List of available records and extent of validation made on the accounts, and (b) Certification and reasons why the books of accounts/records, financial statements/schedules and supporting voucher/documents cannot be located pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-001 dated February 5, 1997.

7. We recommended that the City Accountant exert effort to retrace the transactions prior to June 30, 2016 to support the noted balances due to GSIS, PAG-Ibig, and PhilHealth and make the necessary adjustment/reconciliation thereof to facilitate the immediate remittance of the same to the concerned agencies. Initiate measures to facilitate reconciliation of records with the concerned agencies.

8. Cause the immediate submission of a complete list of all PPAs in CY 2013, thus resulting in the delays in the reporting and
validation of projects.
9. Provide the Audit Team with updated information as to the status of the publicity of PPAs; and
10. Strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.

11. We recommended that management should furnish COA copy of any contract/ purchase order including all pertinent supporting documents as enumerated under COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 within five (5) days after its perfection / approval / execution.
12. We recommended that the required annual LDRRMFIP for CY 2018 be prepared in adherence to the provisions COA Circular No. 2012-002 and submitted to the Auditor for our review and evaluation.

13 We recommended that the City Accountant be required to facilitate the deposit and transfer of unexpended/ unobligated balance of the QRF and MOOE as of December 31, 2018 in the special trust account under the Trust Liability- DRRM account in the Trust Fund books of accounts in compliance with R.A. No. 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002.

14. We recommended that the City should comply with the statutory requirement of allocating at least five percent (5%) of the total appropriation to ensure that GAD plan and budget is provided with adequate resources to carry out GAD activities.

15. We recommended that the Management should furnish the Auditor a copy of the GAD Accomplishment Report with the list of programs, projects and activities where these are mainstreamed for validation purposes.

16. We recommended that the City Accountant and the City Treasurer facilitate the immediate submission of all unsubmitted DVs and their supporting documents to the Auditor to avoid the issuance of notice of suspension/disallowance and to strictly comply with the prescribed mode of submission.

17. We recommended that the City Accountant and the City Treasurer facilitate the immediate submission of all identified unsubmitted RCDs and their supporting documents to the Auditor, and
18. to strictly comply with the prescribed mode of submission as provided under Section 7.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006.

19. We recommended that Management properly screen the project proposals and prioritize development projects that are readily implementable without unresolved issues to prevent delays in the implementation and completion of projects.
20. We recommended that the City Budget Officer and the City Accountant prepare the SCBAA as of December 31, 2018 and submit the report to COA for review.

21. Require the City Accountant to make a detailed review of the unsupported dormant account balances reflected in the books of accounts and effect the necessary adjustments where appropriate and furnish the COA with a copy of any relevant record containing sufficient information about the foregoing account balances. Make a request for write-off for those account balances that can no longer be accounted for or settled together with a valid justification on the reason that the said account balances should be written off, such as (a) List of available records and extent of validation made on the accounts, and (b) Certification and reasons why the books of accounts/records, cannot be located.

Require the City Accountant and the CDRRMO to establish the amount of unexpended/ unobligated balance of the QRF and MOOE as of December 31, 2017 and facilitate the deposit and transfer to the special trust fund accounts.

Require the City Accountant to make a detailed review of the unsupported dormant account balances reflected in the books of accounts and effect the necessary adjustments where appropriate and furnish the COA with a copy of any relevant record containing sufficient information about the foregoing account balances. Make a request for write-off for those account balances that can no longer be accounted for or settled together with a valid justification on the reason that the said account balances should be written off, such as (a) List of available records and extent of validation made on the accounts, and (b) Certification and reasons why the books of accounts/records, FS/ schedules and supporting voucher/ documents cannot be located.
Direct the City Budget Officer and the City Accountant prepare the SCBAA as of December 31, 2017 and submit the report to COA for review.

1.8 We recommended that the City Mayor direct:
a. the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of PPE; prepare the report using the Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE): and submit a copy to the Audit Team.
b. the City Accountant to update all property records; maintain PPE Ledger Card for each category of PPE. Such ledger cards shall contain the details of the PPE account in the inventory control account in the general ledger to ensure accurate presentation of PPE in the FS.
c. the CGSO to maintain Property Cards (PC) and Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) for properties and equipment to account for the receipt and disposition of the same to establish custodianship and to determine accountability.
d. the City Accountant and the CGSO to reconcile their records with the physical count report.

2.5. We recommended that the City Accountant make a detailed review of the unsupported dormant account balance reflected in the books of accounts and effect the necessary adjustments where appropriate and furnish the COA with a copy of any relevant record containing sufficient information about the foregoing account balances.

2.6. We further recommended that Management make a request for write-off for the account balance with a valid justification on the reason that the said account balances should be written off, such as (a) List of available records and extent of validation made on the accounts, and (b) Certification and reasons why the books of accounts/records, financial statements/schedules and supporting voucher/documents cannot be located pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-001 dated February 5, 1997.

3.6. We recommended that the City Accountant exert effort to retrace the transactions prior to June 30, 2016 to support the noted balances due to GSIS, PAG-Ibig, and PhilHealth and make the necessary adjustment/reconciliation thereof to facilitate the immediate remittance of the same to the concerned agencies. Initiate measures to facilitate reconciliation of records with the concerned agencies.

4.6. We recommended that Management :
a. Cause the immediate submission of a complete list of all PPA in CY 2018;
b. Provide the Audit Team with updated information as to the status of the publicity of PPA; and
c. Strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular 2013-004.

5.5. We recommended that Management furnish COA copy of any contract/PO including all pertinent supporting documents as enumerated under COA Circular 2009-001 within five (5) days after its perfection / approval / execution.

6.6. We recommended that Management require the annual LDRRMFIP for CY 2018 in adherence to the provisions COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012, and submitted to the Auditor for review and evaluation.

7.4. We recommended that the City Accountant facilitate the deposit and transfer of unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and MOOE as of December 31, 2018 in the special trust account under the Trust Liability-DRRM account in the Trust Fund books of accounts.

8.4. We recommended that the City should comply with the statutory requirement of allocating at least five percent (5%) of the total appropriation to ensure that GAD plan and budget is provided with adequate resources to carry out GAD activities.
9.5. We recommended that Management furnish the Auditor a copy of the GAD Accomplishment Report with the list of PPA where these are mainstreamed for validation purposes.

10.6. We recommended that the City Accountant and the City Treasurer facilitate the immediate submission of all identified unsubmitted DVs and their supporting documents to the Auditor to avoid the issuance of notice of suspension/disallowance and to strictly comply with the prescribed mode of submission as provided under Section 44 of MNGAS, Volume I and Section 7.2.1 (a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006.

11.4. We recommended that the City Accountant and the City Treasurer facilitate the immediate submission of all identified unsubmitted RCDs and their supporting documents to the Auditor and to strictly comply with the prescribed mode of submission as provided under Section 7.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006.

12.8. We recommended that Management properly screen the project proposals and prioritize the development projects that are readily implementable without unresolved issues to prevent delays in the implementation and completion of projects.
13.5. We recommended that the City Budget Officer and the City Accountant prepare the SCBAA as of December 31, 2018 and submit the report to COA for review.
16.4. We recommended that Management require the persons responsible to immediately settle and comply with the requirements indicated in the audit suspensions.

Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of PPE; prepare the inventory report using the RPCPPE and submit a copy to the Audit Team, the City Accountant to update all property records; maintain PPE Ledger Card for each category of PPE containing the details of the PPE accounts in the inventory control account in the general ledger, the CGSO to maintain Property Cards and ARE for properties and equipment to account for the receipt and disposition of the same to establish custodianship and to determine accountability, and the City Accountant and the CGSO to reconcile their records with the physical count report.

Require the City DRRMO to submit to the Auditor the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF, duly certified by the City Accountant, as well as the periodic reports required under COA Circular No. 2012-002 and COA Circular No. 2014-002.
Require the City Accountant to record the said receipts in the books of accounts and submit to the Auditor the related JEV.
Draw a Program of Work for the efficient utilization of the cash donation so that the funds will cater to the immediate needs of those affected constituents.

Comply with the statutory appropriation of 5% of estimated revenue from regular sources to obtain sufficient funds to carry out the disaster risk management activities and to attain the objectives of RA 10121.

Comply with the statutory requirement of allocating at least five percent (5%) of the total appropriation to ensure that GAD plan and budget is provided with adequate resources to carry out GAD activities.
Direct the City Planning and Development Officer to be directed to furnish the Auditor a copy of the Accomplishment Report for GAD showing the PPA undertaken during the year.
Furnish the copy of any contract/ PO to COA including all pertinent supporting documents within five (5) days after its perfection /approval /execution.
Comply with the statutory appropriation of 1% of IRA to obtain sufficient funds to carry out the PPA of the LCPC.
Require the persons responsible to immediately settle and comply with the requirements indicated in the audit suspensions.

a) Direct the inventory committee to complete their physical count report using the RPCPPE and submit a copy to the Audit Team.; b) Direct the CGSO and Accountant  to secure relevant documents that will substantiate and establish pertinent information leading to the completeness of the physical count reports; c) Direct the City Accountant to update all property records; maintain PPE Ledger Card for each category of PPE. Such ledger cards shall contain the details of the PPE accounts in the inventory control account in the general ledger ensure accurate presentation of PPE in the financial statements; d) Direct the CGSO to maintain Property Cards (PC) & ARE for properties and equipment to account for the receipt and disposition of the same to establish custodianship and to determine accountability; and to reconcile their records with the physical count report.

Require the City Accountant to make a detailed review of the unsupported dormant account balances reflected in the books of accounts and effect the necessary adjustments where appropriate and furnish the COA with a copy of any relevant record containing sufficient information about the foregoing account balances. Make a request for write-off for those account balances that can no longer be accounted for or settled together with a valid justification on the reason that the said account balances should be written off, such as (a) List of available records and extent of validation made on the accounts, and (b)Certification and  reasons why the books of accounts/records, financial statements/schedules  and supporting voucher/documents cannot be located.

Require the City Accountant to determine the unexpended balances for QRF and MOOE and shall be transferred to a special trust fund under the account Trust Liability-DRRM in the Trust Fund books to be used solely for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities of the City for the next five (5) years in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002.

a) Require the completion of the physical count report; b) Instruct the CGSO to officially request the GSIS to conduct evaluation of properties for the consideration in the insurance plan coverage; c) Direct the CGSO to coordinate with the City Budget Officer in the estimation of premiums that may be incurred for the insurance plan; and; d) Advise the City Budget Officer to appropriate the necessary amount to cover for the payment of premiums for the insurance of the properties in annual budget. 
Comply with R.A. No. 656 (Property Insurance Law) to ensure that all identified insurable assets belonging to the City Government are adequately covered/insured with the GIF of the GSIS.

Direct the City Mayor should direct the City Accountant to update the accounting records and come up with a detailed list or matrix comprising the unremitted obligations as of June 30, 2016 and to settle said obligations.

Furnish COA copy of any contract/ POs including all pertinent supporting documents as enumerated under COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 within five (5) days after its perfection / approval / execution.

a) Strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 to enhance transparency on its operations and project implementation; b) Furnish the Audit Team the necessary information within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity and post signboards and/or public notices in the project site for the timely validation.

Register with the PHILGEPS.  Designate officials or personnel authorized to transact with and operate the PHILGEPS from their terminals pursuant to IRR-A of R.A. 9184.  Seek the assistance of the PS-DBM to ensure their on-line connectivity and for the training of their personnel responsible for the operation of PHILGEPS.

Adherence to the prescribed regulations in preparing monthly report of accountability for accountable forms in order to establish the accountability of the accountable officers on a monthly basis.
Advise the City Treasurer to prepare and submit to the Audit Team the MRAAF in the prescribed form.

Direct the City Accountant, City Treasurer and accountable officers to strictly observe the rules and regulations on the submission of reports.  The City Treasurer’s Office should submit the RCIs, RCDs together with its paid DVs/payrolls,  official receipts and deposit slips daily to the accounting office. The City Accountant should record entries in the Journals on a daily basis or as soon as the financial documents are submitted by the City Treasurer’s Office to prevent delay in the preparation of financial statements. Further, require the City Accountant to submit the reports/documents to the Audit Team within the first 10 days of the ensuing month.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct:
the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of PPE; prepare the inventory report using the Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE): and submit a copy to the Audit Team.
the City Accountant to update all property records; maintain PPE Ledger Card for each category of PPE.  Such ledger cards shall contain the details of the PPE account in the inventory control account in the general ledger to ensure accurate presentation of PPE in the financial statements.
the CGSO to maintain Property Cards (PC) and Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) for properties and equipment to account for the receipt and disposition of the same to establish custodianship and to determine accountability.
the City Accountant and the CGSO to reconcile their records with the physical count report.

We recommended that Management require the City DRRMO to submit to the Auditor the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF, duly certified by the City Accountant, as well as the periodic reports required Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014.
The City Accountant and the City DRRM Officer committed to comply with the submission of the periodic reports.

We recommended that Management require the City Accountant and the CDRRMO to establish the amount of unexpended/unobligated balance of the QRF and MOOE as of December 31, 2017 and facilitate the deposit and transfer to the special trust fund accounts.
Management commented that the unexpended balance for QRF and MOOE as of December 31, 2016  and 2017 were not transferred to the special trust fund account because these were utilized for the relief  and recovery operations conducted during the siege.
Management further committed to transfer the unexpended/unobligated balance of Calamity Fund in the ensuing year.

We recommended that Management require the City Accountant to record the said receipts in the books of accounts and submit to the Auditor the related Journal Entry Voucher (JEV).
The City Treasurer admitted the deficiency and explained that the Report of Collections and Deposit for the subject cash donations was prepared but failed to submit to the City Accountant. He stated that the said donations were received in checks and are still intact. He further stated that these were already deposited in the bank in January 2018.

We recommended that Management should draw a Program of Work for the efficient utilization of the cash donation so that the funds will cater to the immediate needs of those affected constituents.
Management explained that the donations were used mostly for the provision of relief goods for the evacuees and the remaining amounts were programmed for rehabilitation and recovery programs and other forms of assistance to address the needs of those who were greatly affected by the calamity.
Management further assured the Audit Team that cash donations are utilized in accordance with the specific intents of the donors.

We recommended that the Management should comply with the statutory appropriation of 5% of estimated revenue from regular sources to obtain sufficient funds to carry out the disaster risk management activities and to attain the objectives of RA 10121.
Management commented to comply with the recommendation.

We recommended that the City should comply with the statutory requirement of allocating at least five percent (5%) of the total appropriation to ensure that GAD plan and budget is provided with adequate resources to carry out GAD activities.
Management commented to comply with the recommendation.

We recommended that the Management direct the City Planning and Development Officer be directed to furnish the Auditor a copy of the Accomplishment Report for GAD showing the programs, projects and activities undertaken during the year.
The City Planning and Development Officer committed to comply with the requirements.

We recommended that Management furnish the copy of any contract/ purchase order to COA including all pertinent supporting documents within five (5) days after its perfection /approval /execution.
Management admitted the deficiency and committed to comply with the requirements.

We recommended that the City Government comply with the statutory appropriation of 1% of IRA to obtain sufficient funds to carry out the PPA of the Local Councils for the Protection of Children (LCPC).
Management committed to comply with the requirements.

We recommended that the City Budget Officer and the City Accountant prepare the SCBAA as of December 31, 2017 and submit the report to COA for review.
The City Accountant and the City Budget Officer promised to prepare and submit the said report.
We recommended that Management require the persons responsible to immediately settle and comply with the requirements indicated in the audit suspensions.

Require the City Accountant and her staff  to submit on time the necessary year-end trial balance together with the required supporting and/or subsidiary statements thereon in accordance with the Philippine Public Sector Accounting System.

We recommend that the management shall include in their CY 2015 action plan to be registered with PhilGeps to be able to fully utilize the PhiGeps and meets its objective on promoting transparency and efficiency in procurement process and to give all interested parties an equal opportunity to bid.

We recommend that the management observe/ adhere to the guidelines stipulated in COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 Re: Information and Publicity of Programs, Projects and Activities of government agencies.  This is to promote Good Governance through transparency and accountability.

Management should submit the required/ necessary documents for the legal and technical review as mandated in R.A. No. 9184.  Management should request for inspection of the various projects and enhance cooperation and support to the COA inspectorate of the said project by guiding them to the project site. The City Planning and Development Coordinator should strictly follow guidelines mandated in the DILG and DBM Circulars pertaining to the 20% Economic Development Fund.  The City Accountant must separate the Liquidation Report of each fund for proper evaluation thereof.

We recommend that management particularly to the members of the BAC to strictly comply with the aforesaid regulations.  In cases of procurement method applied not within the approved APP, the BAC through a resolution must recommend to the Head of the Procuring Agency the specific alternative method of procurement to be used and secure the prior approval, before resorting to any alternative method of procurement pursuant to Sec. 48.1 and 48.3 of the revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommend that copies of POs/Contracts and Acceptance and Inspection Reports be submitted to the Auditor’s Office as required under Sec. (1-2) of COA Circular No. 96-010 and Section 118 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: No copies of PO/contracts were submitted

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly adhere with COA circular 74-34, 87-278 and 2009-001.  Perfected contracts must be submitted with the Office of the Audit Team Leader for proper review and evaluation thereof.
observations.managementAction: No copies of PO/contracts were submitted

observations.recommendation: Reevaluate policy on the hiring of contractual workers. Reassess capability of the existing human resources. Discontinue contract of service which might be found unnecessary and strictly comply with the requisites of law on procurement of manpower.
Management should submit to the auditor concerned all documents pertaining to the loan as soon as possible for proper evaluation and examination.
observations.managementAction: No loan documents submitted by the previous administration

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct:
the inventory committee to complete their inventory report using the Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and submit a copy to the Audit Team.
the CGSO and Accounting offices secure relevant documents that will substantiate and establish pertinent information leading to the completeness of the inventory reports.
The City Accountant to update all property records; maintain PPE Ledger Card for each category of property, plant and equipment.  Such ledger cards shall contain the details of the property, plant and equipment account in the inventory control account in the general ledger ensure accurate presentation of PPEs in the financial statements.
the General Services Officer to maintain Property Cards (PC) and Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) for properties and equipment to account for the receipt and disposition of the same to establish custodianship and to determine accountability.
the City Accountant and the City General Service Officer to reconcile their records with the physical inventory report.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the City Accountant to make a detailed review of the unsupported dormant account balances reflected in the books of accounts and effect the necessary adjustments where appropriate and furnish the COA with a copy of any relevant record containing sufficient information about the foregoing account balances.
Make a request for write-off for those account balances that can no longer be accounted for or settled together with a valid justification on the reason that the said account balances should be written off, such as (a) List of available records and extent of validation made on the accounts, and (b) Certification and                  reasons why the books of accounts/records, financial statements/schedules                 and supporting voucher/documents cannot be located pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-001 dated February 5, 1997.

Recommendation:
Require the City Accountant to determine the unexpended balances for                     QRF and MOOE and shall be transferred to a special trust fund under the account Trust Liability-DRRM in the Trust Fund books to be used solely for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities of the City for the next five (5) years in compliance with COA Circular dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the City Mayor should:
require the completion of the physical inventory report;
instruct the City General Services Officer to officially request the GSIS to conduct evaluation of properties for the consideration in the insurance plan coverage
direct the City General Services Officer to coordinate with the City Budget Officer in the estimation of premiums that may be incurred for the insurance plan; and
advise the City Budget Officer to appropriate the necessary amount to cover for the payment of premiums for the insurance of the properties in annual budget.
We further recommended strict compliance with R.A. No. 656 (Property Insurance Law) to ensure that all identified insurable assets belonging to the City Government are adequately covered/insured with the GIF of the GSIS.

We recommended that the City Mayor should direct the City Accountant to update the accounting records and come up with a detailed list or matrix comprising the unremitted obligations as of June 30, 2016 and to settle said obligations.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management should furnish COA copy of any contract/ purchase order including all pertinent supporting documents as enumerated under COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 within five (5) days after its perfection / approval / execution.

We recommended that Management:
Strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013 to enhance transparency on its operations and project implementation.
Furnish the Audit Team the necessary information within ten (10) days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity and post signboards and/or public notices in the project site for the timely validation.

We have recommended that to be able to use the PHILGEPS, the agency should register with the PHILGEPS.  Designate officials or personnel authorized to transact with and operate the PHILGEPS from their terminals pursuant to IRR-A of R.A. 9184.  Seek the assistance of the PS-DBM to ensure their on-line connectivity and for the training of their personnel responsible for the operation of PHILGEPS.

We recommended strict adherence to the prescribed regulations in preparing monthly report of accountability for accountable forms in order to establish the accountability of the accountable officers on a monthly basis.
Advise the City Treasurer to prepare and submit to the Audit Team the MRAAF in the prescribed form.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the City Accountant, City Treasurer and accountable officers to strictly observe the rules and regulations on the submission of reports.  The City Treasurer’s Office should submit the RCIs, RCDs together with its paid vouchers/payrolls, official receipts and deposit slips daily to the accounting office. The City Accountant should record entries in the Journals on a daily basis or as soon as the financial documents are submitted by the City Treasurer’s Office to prevent delay in the preparation of financial statements. Further, require the City Accountant to submit the reports/documents to the Audit Team within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month.
It is recommended that all concerned accountable officers must strictly observe/ adhere to the aforesaid rules and regulations of maintaining an updated Cashbook.
We recommend that management should reconcile with the respective agencies to determine their unpaid balances and make full remittances thereto in accordance with the above cited laws.
observations.managementAction: The new administration complies with the regular remittances to BIR, GSIS, Pag-Ibig and PhilHealth.
observations.reason: Amounts withheld as of June 30, 2016  by the previous administration were not yet remitted.

observations.recommendation: Strict compliance with laws, rules and regulations of LDRRMF implementation is hereby required and submit Accomplishment Report thereof to the Auditor concerned for proper evaluation. The City Accountant should set aside a subsidy to other funds and transfer prior years unexpended and or unobligated balance of the LDRRMF to the Trust books using Trust Liability-DRRM.  The City Budget Officer should likewise maintain subsidiary ledger and records of the appropriation, expenditures and balances of the LDRRMF for the current year and previous years.
observations.managementAction:
No specific plans submitted yet.
Accomplishment reports are submitted

observations.recommendation: Management should appropriate 5% of its total appropriation for GAD Fund. Management should formulate Plans and Programs for GAD activities. Management should submit GAD Accomplishment Report to the auditor concerned.

Prioritize the initiation of physical count on all PPE. 

Create an Inventory Committee to take charge of the physical count on PPE and submit corresponding reports to the Office of the Auditor.

The Accounting Office and General Services Office/ Treasury Office shall likewise maintain and update an Equipment Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively and often times reconcile balances of each cards maintained by them and with the balances in the Report on Physical Count to be able to reflect complete and accurate PPE balance in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: New administration conducted the physical inventory but failed to submit complete the report
observations.reason: Accounting and property records were not maintained.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the city accountant and the city treasurer be instructed to strictly adhere the guidelines pertaining to the submission of financial statements & disbursement vouchers and other accounting reports.  Instruct the head of the accounting section to maximize work assignment among accounting personnel, to prevent work overload and meet deadlines on report submission. Demand in writing, from the agency’s Collecting Officer and Disbursing Officer to immediately prepare monthly reports of their transactions and to submit the same together with its supporting documents, to the accounting section within the time prescribed by the regulations.
observations.managementAction: Submission of reports are still delayed

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the city accountant, city engineer and all concerned officials to maintain respective records as assigned and establish the correct account balances.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant reviewed her records

observations.recommendation: No recommendation
No recommendation
observations.reason: The new administration has initiated to comply with the Circular

observations.recommendation: No recommendations
